mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive following months of INDPRO growth (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates discount rates and debt service costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and the consumer price index signal stagflationary pressure that erodes corporate margins and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month and CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP predicts an impending earnings contraction that causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle periods, two consecutive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1.5 million annualized units signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for durable goods, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1500, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with declining industrial production generate a credit squeeze that amplifies earnings downside, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and falling INDPRO fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption pullbacks, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in three months, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 50% of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index forces upward revisions to terminal rate expectations, tightening financial conditions and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration show positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.
